title: Oryctanthus tehuacanensis
intro: Oryctanthus tehuacanensis, also known as Mexican Oryctanthus, is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Oryctanthus tehuacanensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oryctanthus tehuacanensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments to support immune function and alleviate respiratory distress.
taxonomy: Oryctanthus tehuacanensis belongs to the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. It is native to Mexico and is part of the genus Oryctanthus. The classification of Oryctanthus tehuacanensis further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Further taxonomic classification is also possible at the order Gentianales level.
